title: Login redirect loop after SSO change
body:
We recently switched our company's SSO provider from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. Since the switch, some users are getting stuck in an infinite redirect loop when trying to log into TaskFlow. They click login, get redirected to Microsoft, authenticate successfully, get redirected back to TaskFlow, and then immediately get sent back to Microsoft again. Not all users are affected — maybe 30% of our team. The rest can log in fine.

We've already verified that the Entra ID app registration is correct and matches our redirect URIs. The affected users can log into other apps that use the same SSO just fine.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed write-up — the redirect loop behavior is clear and helpful.

Could you share some environment details? Specifically:
- What **browser(s)** are the affected users using? And do the unaffected users happen to be on a different browser?
- What **version of TaskFlow** are you running?
- Is TaskFlow self-hosted or cloud-hosted, and if self-hosted, what's the server environment (OS, runtime version)?

Since only ~30% of users are affected, a browser or client-side difference could be a key clue here.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: We're on TaskFlow v2.3.1, self-hosted. As for browsers, the affected users are on a mix — Chrome, Edge, and Firefox. I don't think it's a browser thing because I've seen both affected and unaffected users on Chrome. I haven't looked into the server environment details much, I'd have to check with our dev team on the exact OS and runtime. One thing I did notice though is that the affected users seem to be the ones who had plus signs in their email addresses (like jane+taskflow@company.com) or people whose emails were set up as aliases when we moved to Entra. Not sure if that's relevant but it stood out to me.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That observation about plus signs and email aliases is a really strong clue — thank you for flagging it.

Before we wrap up triage, could you (or your dev team) check the **server-side logs** from TaskFlow during one of the failed login attempts? Specifically, any error messages or warnings related to the authentication callback or session creation would be very helpful. If TaskFlow writes logs to a file or stdout, even the last few lines around the time of a failed login would tell us a lot — especially whether the app is rejecting the token, failing to match the user, or something else entirely.

If you're not sure where the logs are or don't have access, just let me know and we can proceed without them.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I don't have direct access to the server logs myself — I'm more on the IT admin side than the dev side. I can ask our dev team to pull those, but it might take a day or two. What I can tell you is that when I watched one of the affected users try to log in with browser dev tools open, I could see a Set-Cookie header coming back on the redirect from Microsoft, but the cookie didn't seem to stick. The next request to TaskFlow didn't have the session cookie, so it just kicked them back to login. I'm not sure why the cookie isn't being saved though. Also, clearing all cookies and trying again in incognito sometimes works for a bit, which is weird.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"SSO redirect loop after Okta-to-Entra migration for users with plus-sign or alias emails","problem":"After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop during login. Authentication at the Entra side succeeds, but the session cookie returned by TaskFlow does not persist, causing the app to redirect back to Entra repeatedly. The affected population correlates strongly with users who have plus signs in their email addresses (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) or whose emails were set up as aliases during the Entra migration.","root_cause_hypothesis":"TaskFlow's authentication callback likely performs a user lookup or session binding using the email claim from the OIDC token. When Entra returns an email with a plus sign or an alias form, TaskFlow either fails to match it to the existing user record (causing session creation to silently fail) or incorrectly encodes the email in the session cookie value (causing cookie rejection). The Set-Cookie header is present but the cookie does not persist, suggesting the cookie value or attributes may be malformed — possibly due to unencoded special characters from the plus-sign email leaking into the cookie path or value.","reproduction_steps":["Set up TaskFlow v2.3.1 with Microsoft Entra ID SSO","Create or use a user account whose email contains a plus sign (e.g., user+tag@company.com) or is an alias in Entra","Attempt to log in via SSO","Observe: authentication succeeds at Entra, redirect back to TaskFlow occurs, but session cookie does not persist and user is immediately redirected back to Entra in a loop","Compare with a user whose email has no plus sign or alias — login should succeed normally"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3.1, self-hosted. Server OS and runtime version unknown. Multiple browsers affected (Chrome, Edge, Firefox) — not browser-specific.","severity":"high","impact":"Approximately 30% of users are completely unable to log in to TaskFlow. These users are locked out with no reliable workaround (incognito mode works intermittently). This is blocking daily work for a significant portion of the team.","recommended_fix":"1. Inspect the authentication callback handler — check how the email claim from the Entra OIDC token is used for user lookup. Look for exact-match comparisons that would fail when the email format differs between Okta and Entra (e.g., plus-sign encoding, alias vs. primary email). 2. Check whether the session cookie value is being set with unencoded special characters from the email. 3. Add email normalization (case-insensitive, plus-sign-aware matching) or match on a stable claim like `sub` or `oid` rather than email. 4. Verify that the redirect URI callback properly handles all email formats returned by Entra.","proposed_test_case":"Write an integration test that performs an SSO login flow with an email containing a plus sign (user+tag@domain.com). Assert that the session cookie is set correctly, persists across requests, and the user is not redirected back to the identity provider after the callback completes.","information_gaps":["Exact server OS and runtime version (reporter needs to check with dev team — unlikely to change the fix direction)","Server-side logs from a failed login attempt (would confirm whether the failure is in user lookup, token validation, or session creation)","Whether the emails stored in TaskFlow's user database still have the Okta-era format vs. the Entra-era format"]}
